channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Zimbabwe’s sole lithium salt plant unable to process third-party metal, official says @Reuters
item.description: Zimbabwe’s sole lithium salt plant unable to process third-party metal, official says #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: ROSTER NAMED FOR CANADA’S MEN’S RUGBY TEAM’S NATIONS CUP MATCH AGAINST ZIMBABWE

item.dc:creator: Rugby Canada

item.title: Zimbabwe Reunion Plan Unravels As SA Woman Is Turned Away By Husband’s Family
item.description: Mother And Three Children Left Waiting After Moving To Zimbabwe A South African woman’s hopes of starting a new life with her husband in Zimbabwe have been dashed after his family allegedly refused to take her in. Charlotte Masemola, 34, travelled from Mpumalanga to Zimbabwe with #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: ZimParks Urges Zimbabweans To Stop Killing Snakes As Human Encounters Increase
item.description: The Zimbabwe Parks and Wildlife Management Authority (ZimParks) has urged Zimbabweans to stop killing snakes indiscriminately, warning that encounters between people and the reptiles are likely to increase as human settlements continue expanding into natural habitats. The appeal #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Slain UK Mum’s Family Break Silence: ‘I Wish I Had Told Her To Come Home’
item.description: Family Of Zimbabwean Mum Killed In UK Pleads For Privacy The family of a Zimbabwean woman allegedly killed alongside her two daughters in the United Kingdom has broken its silence. Grieving relatives say they are devastated by the loss of Nothabo Zandile Tshuma and have pleaded w #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Zimbabwe Rejects Calls To Expel 25,000 South Africans As Ziyambi Defends Ubuntu
item.description: Zimbabwe Makes Surprise Decision After Calls To Expel South Africans Zimbabwe has rejected calls to expel an estimated 25,000 South African citizens in response to the growing number of Zimbabweans returning home from South Africa. Justice, Legal and Parliamentary Affairs Minister Ziyambi Ziyambi told Parliament the government would continue welcoming foreign nationals who are legally [...] The post Zimbabwe Rejects Calls To Expel 25,000 South Africans As Ziyambi Defends Ubuntu appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Mutapa Gold Resources unveils US$212 million expansion plan to lift output
item.description: ‘We must grow the business to make sure it makes its fair contribution to the Zimbabwean economy’ The post Mutapa Gold Resources unveils US$212 million expansion plan to lift output appeared first on Zimbabwe News Now. #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwean News

item.title: Murowa Diamonds placed under corporate rescue as RioZim debt battle intensifies
item.description: ‘This is a diamond mine that must not be allowed to deteriorate beyond a certain point’ The post Murowa Diamonds placed under corporate rescue as RioZim debt battle intensifies appeared first on Zimbabwe News Now. #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwean News

item.title: Varun’s Reported Dairibord Interest Signals Strategic Pivot as Beverage Growth Slows, Analysts Say
item.description: HARARE – Varun Beverages’ reported interest in acquiring a controlling stake in Dairibord Holdings is increasingly being viewed by business analysts as a strategic response to mounting pressure on its core beverages business, rather than simply another expansion opportunity. Writing on his X platform, business analyst Tinashe Mukogo of Money & Moves argued that the [...] The post Varun’s Reported Dairibord Interest Signals Strategic Pivot as Beverage Growth Slows, Analysts Say appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: SA court orders ‘Nyokayemabhunu’ extradition despite persecution fears @zimsituation
item.description: Court says only the justice minister can halt extradition Source: SA court orders ‘Nyokayemabhunu’ extradition despite persecution fears – Zimbabwe News Now Wellington Masiwa JOHANNESBURG, South Africa – A South African court has cleared the way for the extradition of Zimbabwean political activist Wellington Masiwa, widely known as Nyokayemabhunu, rejecting his arguments that he risks [...] The post SA court orders ‘Nyokayemabhunu’ extradition despite persecution fears appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe Situation

item.title: Opposition parties condemn arrest of Beitbridge activist Bhekimpilo Mbedzi @zimsituation
item.description: Source: Opposition parties condemn arrest of Beitbridge activist Bhekimpilo Mbedzi — CITEZW Opposition political parties have condemned the arrest of Beitbridge-based activist Bhekimpilo Mbedzi, describing it as an attack on constitutional rights and calling on authorities to either release him immediately or ensure his legal rights are protected. Mbedzi, the Matabeleland South chairperson of the [...] The post Opposition parties condemn arrest of Beitbridge activist Bhekimpilo Mbedzi appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe Situation

item.title: From Jail to the State House: The Shocking Rise of Dilesh Nguwaya as President Mnangagwa’s New Advisor
item.description: In the high-stakes theatre of Zimbabwean politics, the line between a prison cell and the corridors of power has often proven to be remarkably thin. However, few ascensions have been as rapid or as brazen as that of Dilesh Nguwaya. Once the face of a multi-million dollar medical procurement scandal that crippled the nation’s pandemic [...] The post From Jail to the State House: The Shocking Rise of Dilesh Nguwaya as President Mnangagwa’s New Advisor first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: No tit-for-tat as Zimbabwe govt rejects calls to expel 25,000 South Africans @nehandaradio
item.description: Zimbabwe has ruled out retaliating against South Africa by expelling South African citizens, insisting it will uphold Ubuntu while assisting thousands of returning Zimbabweans. The post No tit-for-tat as Zimbabwe govt rejects calls to expel 25,000 South Africans appeared first on Nehanda Radio. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Nehanda Radio

item.title: ZCTU slams arrest of council members as NPA declines prosecution
item.description: Five Zimbabwe Congress of Trade Unions (ZCTU) General Council (GC) members arrested earlier this week were released on Wednesday after the National Prosecuting Authority (NPA) declined to prosecute them, the country’s labour body has said. The development comes amid a deepening leadership dispute within the ZCTU following last week’s suspension of Secretary-General Tirivanhu Marimo by [...] The post ZCTU slams arrest of council members as NPA declines prosecution appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Holy Ten Baby Mama Chelsea Tariro Hlomayi Makes Disturbing Abuse Allegations Against Boyfriend
item.description: Chelsea Tariro Hlomayi Accuses New Boyfriend Of Shocking Abuse Chelsea Tariro Hlomayi, the mother of one of rapper Holy Ten’s children, has accused her new boyfriend of verbal and physical abuse after breaking her silence on social media. In an emotional Facebook post shared on Thursday, 16 July 2026, Chelsea detailed the allegations, shared photographs [...] The post Holy Ten Baby Mama Chelsea Tariro Hlomayi Makes Disturbing Abuse Allegations Against Boyfriend appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: 2028 Elections CANCELLED: The Most Dangerous Move in Zimbabwe’s History
item.description: The signing of the Constitution of Zimbabwe Amendment (No. 3) Act, 2026, has rippled through the nation, but the real story is not just about the numbers; it is about the quiet death of the 2028 general election. By extending the presidential term from five to seven years and making it retroactive, the Second Republic [...] The post 2028 Elections CANCELLED: The Most Dangerous Move in Zimbabwe’s History first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: South African woman stranded in Zimbabwe after following husband @bulawayo24
item.description: A South African woman who travelled to Zimbabwe to reunite with her husband after anti-immigrant unrest in South Africa says she has been left stranded at a transit centre in Bulawayo after his family... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Dilesh Nguwaya’s Circular Economy Mission: Why Zimbabwe’s Next Growth Story Could Be Built from Waste
item.description: AS a newly appointed adviser to the President on environmental policies, Dilesh Nguwaya has an opportunity to move Zimbabwe’s environmental conversation beyond waste disposal and towards a national economic strategy centred on resource efficiency, innovation and sustainable industrial growth. By Brighton Musonza Global investment decisions are increasingly being shaped by environmental performance, with investors paying [...] The post Dilesh Nguwaya’s Circular Economy Mission: Why Zimbabwe’s Next Growth Story Could Be Built from Waste appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail
